data.short_description: A new food shop and café is set to open in the historic Bank of Boston building in Milton Village, vacant since 2020. The market will offer fresh produce, grab-and-go meals, café beverages, and a curated gifts section called The Vault. Plans were approved by the Milton Planning Board last spring.

A new Italian hi-fi bar from Josh Walker of Xiao Bao and Ryan Costanza of Present Tense is heading to East Nashville. The concept will feature natural wines, Italian island cooking, and frozen negronis with a wine list curated by Matthew Bone.

A new lakefront bar and grille is coming to a dock on Cottage Grove Lake near Akron, from hospitality professionals John Logsdon and Steve Baker of Radius Hospitality. The team aims to create a casual, fun dining experience with dishes like steak, pasta, and fish.

Fast-casual Lebanese-inspired chain NAYA is opening an approximately 2,047-square-foot location at Shoppers World in Framingham. Founded in 2008, NAYA has scaled to more than 40 locations across five East Coast states, with additional Boston-area locations planned for 2026.

data.location: Address not specified (Logan Square, Chicago, IL)
data.short_description: SABA Italian Bar + Kitchen, a popular Logan Square spot known for modern Italian cuisine and events like Martini Mondays, permanently closed after eight years. The restaurant was known for dishes like pesto chicken gnocchi and tableside Parmesan cheese wheel pasta.

Max and Issy's Pizzeria, known for New York-style pizza, permanently closed after the owners decided to sell the building housing the restaurant. The closure surprised diners who loved the restaurant's creative pies and casual neighborhood atmosphere.

data.location: Address not specified (Back of the Yards, Chicago, IL)
data.short_description: Whiner Beer Company closed its doors after a 10-year run in Chicago's Back of the Yards community amid broader industry challenges. The closure marks the end of a decade-long run for the brewery.

Wayback Burgers is opening its 13th Georgia location in Kennesaw, operated by husband-and-wife franchisees Zakaria Dhaly and Mahmuda Rumi in a 1,606-square-foot space. The restaurant will feature a modern atmosphere with glass windows into the kitchen and seating options including booths and community tables.

data.location: 110 S Cedar St, Unit C, Summerville, SC 29483
data.short_description: Swank Creamery, an offshoot of Swank Desserts owned by Danetra Richardson, is opening next door to its sister store and will offer scratch-made ice cream using locally sourced dairy, cookies, and espresso. An official opening date has not been set; Richardson recently secured the space and is building it out.

Luce Ristorante e Enoteca, an Italian restaurant serving the San Antonio community for over 20 years, closed after owner and chef Joe Buonincontri announced the business would not renew its lease. The restaurant was known for house-made pasta, seasonal vegetables, and seafood in the style of an Italian enoteca.

data.location: 119 Staffel St, Boerne, TX (zip not specified)
data.short_description: The Creek Restaurant, a seafood-focused fine dining destination in a historic building overlooking Cibolo Creek, closed its doors on March 14. Owner Jean Tardif announced the closure through social media, thanking patrons for their support, though no reason for closure was disclosed.

Everybody Loves Raymond's was among the San Antonio restaurants that closed in March 2026. The article groups it with other closures as part of a broader shift in the region's evolving restaurant landscape.

East Village entrepreneur Abby Ehmann is planning B Scene, a two-level bar and live music club in the former Housewatch space at 50 Avenue B. A formal lease has not yet been signed, as it is contingent on receiving a liquor license approval from Manhattan Community Board 3 at its April 13 meeting.

data.location: 3995 Richmond Ave Suite B, Houston, TX 77027
data.short_description: Mean Grille, a Turkish-Mediterranean restaurant in Houston's Greenway area owned by Onur Palamut, permanently closed after just two years of service on March 30, 2026. The family-owned restaurant was known for fresh, made-to-order dishes like kebabs, falafel, and hummus using locally sourced halal proteins.

data.location: 2907 Fire Rd, Egg Harbor Township, NJ 08234
data.short_description: Fire Golf Club, a private golf club founded by healthcare professionals Adam Saad and Miro Kesic, will open in Egg Harbor Township this summer featuring eight GolfZon simulators and a PuttView putting green. The club will also offer club rental, club fitting services, and individual lessons.

data.status: gearing up to open this spring
data.short_description: B-Side Group is opening Lady Funari, a modern pasta bar and caffè in South End Charlotte, in a 1,600-square-foot space with 40 indoor and 40 outdoor seats. The concept is a tribute to culinary director Vince Giancarlo's grandmother and will feature handmade pastas, locally sourced vegetables, and an Italian beverage program.

Ilios Crafted Greek is planning a new location at Sadler Square Shopping Center in Davidson, as announced on Instagram by co-owner Stratos Lambos. The fast-casual Greek restaurant already has locations in South End, Baxter Village, Oakhurst, and Stonecrest at Piper Glen, and no exact opening timeline has been provided.

data.location: 506 Yale St Suite E, Houston, TX 77007
data.short_description: Be More Pacific, a Filipino kitchen and bar in Houston's Heights neighborhood owned by Mark Pascual and Giovan Cuchapin, permanently closed on March 31, 2026 after six years at the Yale Street location. The restaurant began as a food truck in 2012 and was known for traditional Filipino dishes like Kare Kare and Crispy Spam Musubi.

A new drive-thru Scooter's Coffee location is under construction at 530 S Main Street in Bourbonnais, with franchisee Thanga Pichaikani hoping to open by late summer. This will be Pichaikani's first Scooter's location; construction is reported to be on schedule.

Rock Hill-based Knowledge Perk Coffee Co. is planning a new location in York, SC featuring indoor seating, a drive-thru, and a large outdoor patio — a first for the brand. Currently under construction, the location is expected to open in Q4 2026.

A new Piada Italian Street Food location is planned for Pflugerville's Stone Hill Town Center, with a $500,000 renovation of a 2,450-square-foot space filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation. Work is expected to run from May 4 to July 1.

Aura Dorada, a 100% Latino-owned café-cocktail bar concept from Natalia Martinez and Jorge Andrade, is planned for downtown Portland with no specific address yet revealed. The venue will operate as a café by day and an intimate cocktail bar by night, partnering with local Portland roasters and distilleries.

Sure Shot Burger, a longstanding smashburger food cart, is opening its first brick-and-mortar location at 5011 NE 42nd Ave in Portland's Concordia neighborhood in early June. Owner Nick Jarvis plans to maintain the existing menu while adding beer, wine, milkshakes, and rotating vegetable sides.

Nacho Kat, a Tex-Mex and Latin American restaurant that debuted in Covington in 2023, is preparing to open its first New Orleans location at 3151 Calhoun St in the Uptown neighborhood. The menu will include signature Nacho Kat items alongside dishes and drinks unique to the New Orleans location.

data.event_date: Reopening sometime in May (no exact date)
data.short_description: Upscale European restaurant in downtown Birmingham temporarily closed for renovations, with a planned May reopening. Owner Samy Eid says the restaurant will have a "refreshed look" and a few new menu additions. No exact reopening date has been announced.

data.location: 1536 Fording Island Rd, Suite 107, Hilton Head Island, SC 29926
data.event_date: Grand opening "probably in a month" (no exact date)
data.short_description: Traditional Mexican bakery opening its first brick-and-mortar location in the former Odd Birds space on Hilton Head Island. The bakery offers handmade pastries, bread, sandwiches, and beverages; the grand opening date has not yet been announced.

The Raven Grill, a Houston restaurant open since 1998, permanently closed on March 29, 2026, after the landlord declined to renew the lease. The wood-fired Southwestern restaurant was owned by Sara and Rob Cromie and was known for dishes like pecan-crusted chicken.

Taqueria Cancun, a Houston neighborhood favorite for four decades, shuttered on March 29, 2026. Known for its late-night hours, drive-thru service, and dishes like fajitas, burritos, and tacos, the closure was announced on social media.

data.location: Address not specified (Greenway, River Oaks area)
data.status: announced on social media that it would close
data.short_description: Family-owned Mediterranean restaurant Mean Grille announced on social media it would close on March 30, 2026. Located in the Greenway, River Oaks area, the eatery served Turkish cuisine including traditional kebabs.

data.status: opening soon (marked "Temporarily closed" on Google Maps)
data.short_description: Two longtime franchise operators are launching Boost Coffee & Energy, a new drive-thru coffee concept, with its first location at 7253 103rd St in Jacksonville. The menu features energy drinks, protein lattes, smoothies, coffee, teas, and snacks, using a modular small-footprint drive-thru model.

Big Jakes BBQ is preparing to open at 4906 Soutel Dr in Jacksonville, taking over the former Mr. K Bar-B-Que & Seafood space. The fast-casual restaurant will feature a drive-thru setup and pit-cooked comfort food including smoked meats and homestyle sides.

data.location: 514 S Rampart St, New Orleans, LA 70113
data.status: coming soon (renovations underway per permit filings)
data.short_description: Kandy's is an upcoming restaurant offering classic New Orleans and French-inspired fare in the former Prime NOLA location in the CBD. The space is undergoing renovations including decorative paneling, updated flooring, and modern lighting, with a full beverage program and sit-down dining planned.

data.location: 550 Wells Rd, Suite 1, Orange Park, FL 32073
data.status: looks to be opening (opening timeline unknown)
data.short_description: Paradise Restaurant & Lounge is opening at the Shoppes of Wells Landing on Wells Road in Orange Park, per plan review paperwork submitted by Paradise Restaurant & Lounge LLC. No opening timeline or menu details have been released yet.
Article covers an Applebee's Easter promotional offer (free kids' meals on April 5, 2026) at existing Flynn Group franchise locations in Nevada. No new opening or closure is reported.

data.status: opening timeline still largely under wraps
data.short_description: Mi Tierra Restaurant is opening at 8 N Fourth Street in Haines City, offering Dominican cuisine including sandwiches, hamburgers, and Cuban sandwiches. Further details on the opening timeline are still unavailable; the owner was not immediately available for comment.

A new Chick-fil-A is opening in Lincoln Park at 2060 Southfield Road on April 2, 2026, operated by Detroit native Marcel Smith. The location will offer dine-in, drive-thru, takeout, delivery, and catering, and is expected to create approximately 120 jobs.

Drift Bar, a new upscale courtyard bar at the Sundial shopping complex in St. Petersburg, is moving forward per newly submitted plan review documents. The bar is headed up by the team behind Forbici Modern Italian and is described as a "first-class" establishment.

A new Kaia Bowls location is planned for 1946 Collier Parkway in Lutz, per paperwork submitted by Brook & Grace Enterprise, LLC. The franchise, owned by Marc and Cristinela Ancuta, currently has five locations across Tampa, St. Petersburg, and Clearwater.

data.status: plans moving forward (plan review paperwork submitted)
data.short_description: Pura Vida Miami is planning a new location at 1038 Water Street in Tampa, with a planned 4,209-square-foot indoor space plus outdoor seating. The healthy restaurant chain, founded in 2012, serves sandwiches, wraps, salads, bowls, and all-day breakfast items.

data.location: 857 Collier Rd NW, Suite 11, Atlanta, GA 30318
data.event_date: Late summer or early fall (year not specified)
data.short_description: Franchisee Noah Creighton is planning to open a second Jet's Pizza location in Buckhead at 857 Collier Rd NW, Suite 11, in a 1,160-square-foot space. He is currently in the permitting phase and targeting a late summer or early fall opening, aiming to be the first Jet's Pizza location inside Atlanta proper.

data.location: 104 S Sharon Amity Rd, Charlotte, NC 28211
data.short_description: Columbus, Ohio-based Kitchen Social is planning its first Charlotte location at 104 S. Sharon Amity Road in Cotswold Village, in a 4,200-square-foot space with a 1,000-square-foot patio. The made-from-scratch menu draws from Indian, Asian, Mexican, and Italian cuisines, with a target opening of January 2027.

data.status: No opening date has been announced
data.short_description: PopUp Bagels is planning a 1,500-square-foot shop at Waterford Lakes Town Center in Orlando, next to Jeremiah's Italian Ice. The brand, known for its "grip, rip, and dip" bagel concept, is part of a franchise deal with MPZ Holdings LLC to bring up to 30 Florida locations.

data.status: An opening date has not been announced
data.short_description: Mo' Bettahs Hawaiian Style Food is opening its first Arizona location in Gilbert at the former Carl's Jr. site at 5378 S Power Road. The Utah-founded chain serves Hawaiian plate lunches including kalua pig, pulehu chicken, and Spam musubi.

data.location: Power Rd & Elliot Rd., Gilbert, AZ 85212
data.short_description: Slim Chickens is preparing to break ground at Fry's Plaza on the corner of Power and Elliot Roads in Gilbert, operated by Barnett Management Company under a multi-unit deal for 32 Arizona locations. No opening date has been announced for this location.

data.location: 950 W Peachtree St NW, Atlanta, GA 30309
data.event_date: 4–6 months from article date (no exact date)
data.short_description: Bora Bora Smoothie Cafe is planning its first Georgia location at Plaza Midtown, 950 W. Peachtree St. NW in Atlanta, with franchisee Mohammed Salman currently in the construction phase. The health-focused fast-casual brand offers smoothies, acai, coffee, sandwiches, crepes, and waffles, and currently has 28 locations across 12 states.

Piedmont Beverage Company (a subsidiary of WJ Partners) is planning to open its first Georgia 7 Brew location at 116 Habersham Hills Circle in Cornelia, expected this summer. The coffee chain offers beverages including energy drinks, smoothies, shakes, and fizzes, with more Georgia locations already in the works per state business filings.

data.status: set to open (in the coming months)
data.short_description: Michelin-starred chef Johnny Spero will open fine dining restaurant Passage at The Inn at Langley on Whidbey Island. The inn is undergoing renovations and is set to reopen this summer. No official opening date for Passage has been announced.

Matsunori Handroll Bar is set to open a new location at MarketStreet Lynnfield. Founded in February 2023 by Kevin Liu, the concept is part of NoToro Hospitality Group and offers nearly 30 varieties of hand rolls. No official opening timeline has been announced.

data.location: 536 S Dixie Hwy, Coral Gables, FL 33146
data.short_description: Joe & The Juice filed permit applications to open a new shop at 536 S. Dixie Highway in Coral Gables. No opening date has been announced. The Danish-born chain operates more than 380 locations nationwide and is expanding rapidly in Florida.

A new Chilli's Wings & Fish location is under construction at 3015 Dixie Highway in Hamilton, with signage already up. Owner Ali Iqtaifan stated the opening date will be in June or July and is currently working through construction and permits. This will be the chain's third location.

Fast-casual steakhouse Yukon Standup Steak House will occupy a 1,450-square-foot space in Redmond's Rail District this fall. Founded by Seattle restaurateur Kurt Beecher Dammeier, the concept plans to open in September. Additional details remain under wraps.

data.event_date: Summer (next year per permit; construction July–August 2026)
data.status: anticipated to open by next summer
data.short_description: A new bar called Calico Bar is set to open at 11978 Leslie Rd. in Helotes per a recently filed permit. Construction is slated to begin July 2026 and wrap up in August, with the $650,000 project including a bar, dance floor, stage, and two-floor layout. Owner is Pedro (Pete) Pompa.

Joe & The Juice is opening a 3,100-square-foot location at Waterford Lakes Town Center in Orlando. No opening date has been announced. This is the second Joe & The Juice announced for Central Florida.

data.location: Near Northeastern University, Boston, MA (Address not specified)
data.short_description: Two Saints Tavern, a popular hangout near Northeastern University, permanently closed on March 8, 2026. The bar debuted in 2018 and announced its closure via social media. It was a known gathering spot for students and locals.

O'Connor's Restaurant & Bar permanently closed on March 12, 2026, after 37 years of operation. The Irish pub initially closed temporarily on March 8 due to a burst pipe, and rising operating costs led to the permanent closure. It was a staple in the Worcester community.

Lalo's Mexican Restaurant confirmed its closure during March 2026. It is listed among three Boston-area restaurants that closed that month. No further address or closure date details are provided in the article text.

data.status: seeking a second location / opening this year
data.short_description: Rowdy Beans Coffee is seeking a second location at 3537 Darrow Road in Stow, with signage already up. Owner Julie Feld has not given an opening date as permitting is still in progress. The first location opened in 2022 at 3759 Fishcreek Road in Stow.

data.location: 38 Miller Ave, Suite 9, Mill Valley, CA 94941
data.short_description: Fatma and Berk Kinalilar, owners of Troya Mediterranean Kitchen, are preparing to open a new restaurant concept called Delphine at 38 Miller Avenue, Suite 9, within Mill Creek Plaza. A liquor license application has recently been submitted. No opening date has been confirmed.

data.location: 100 King St W, Toronto, ON M5K 2A1, Canada
data.status: set to move in (exact date not confirmed)
data.short_description: Matcha Haus is expanding to First Canadian Place in the PATH network, taking over the former Chatime Atelier space. This will be the brand's third location and first in the financial district. No exact opening date has been confirmed.

Local Pie, a wood-fired Lowcountry pizzeria owned by Lee Lucier, will open its third location at 135 Parkside Commons in the New Riverside area this spring. The concept follows an "eat where you live" philosophy and uses Mugnaini wood-fired ovens. Prior locations include Hilton Head Island (2015) and Bluffton (2017).

data.status: preparing to open (exact date not announced)
data.short_description: Mt. Diablo BBQ is preparing to open at 3422 Mt. Diablo Boulevard in Lafayette, replacing previous barbecue concepts at that address. The business is owned by Eric Ingram and Lawrence Ingram Jr., who have extensive Northern California barbecue experience. No exact opening date has been announced.

data.location: 285 Enfield Pl, Mississauga, ON L5B 3Y6, Canada
data.short_description: Chiang Mai, a Thai restaurant, is preparing to open its largest location yet at 285 Enfield Place in Mississauga, near Square One Shopping Centre. The company has not officially confirmed the address, and no exact opening date has been announced, but the location is expected to open in April 2026. This will be the brand's sixth location across the GTA.

Labora Cafe, a specialty coffee shop focused on ceremonial grade matcha, is preparing to open its second location at 2899 University Avenue in San Diego's North Park neighborhood. The team is targeting a Fall 2026 debut, around September. The first location opened in late 2025 in the Convoy District.

data.location: 647 Irwin St, Space B, San Rafael, CA 94901
data.event_date: Soft opening end of June; grand opening early July (year not specified)
data.status: soft opening targeted for end of June; grand opening planned for early July
data.short_description: Golfery of Marin, an indoor golf simulator lounge and co-working concept, is preparing to open at 647 Irwin Street, Space B in San Rafael. Co-founders Kelly Jackson and Colin Read are targeting a soft opening at the end of June and a grand opening in early July. The facility will feature five simulator bays, co-working areas, and food and beverage options.

Copper Kettle Brewing Co., founded in 2011 by Jeremy Gobien and Kristen Kozik, announced it will close its Denver location on June 27, 2026, after 15 years of operation. Operations will continue as usual for the next three months, and a 15th anniversary event is planned for June 6–8. The closure was announced via social media.

data.location: 2811 E Prospect Rd., East York, PA 17402
data.short_description: Stack'd Creamery & Burger Bar, owned by young entrepreneur Gavin Binner, will open at 2811 E. Prospect Rd. in York, taking over the former Ripleigh's Creamery space. The menu will feature premium burgers, Ripleigh's ice cream, and cookie dough classics. No specific opening date has been provided.

Frank Pepe Pizzeria Napoletana will permanently close its Plantation, Florida location on April 4, 2026, after five years, as the brand chose not to renew its lease. This was the brand's first Florida location. The brand will continue operating its Delray Beach location and has a new West Palm Beach restaurant expected to open soon.

data.location: 2777 S Las Vegas Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89109
data.short_description: Cantina Contramar, from celebrated Mexican chef Gabriela Cámara, has opened at Fontainebleau Las Vegas. The menu features coastal Mexican flavors including ceviches, aguachiles, Wagyu carne asada, and her signature pescado a la talla. The restaurant was designed by architect Frida Escobedo and marks Cámara's first Las Vegas venture.

non_working.identifier: None** — All 20 articles contained qualifying business opening or closure information and were successfully extracted. Note: Article 5 (Chicago closures) and Article 8 (San Antonio closures) each contained multiple businesses and were split into separate rows accordingly.
